variation_similarity_scores.variation_text: A variable in programming is a named storage location in memory that holds a value which can be changed during program execution. Variables are important because they enable data storage, improve code readability, allow reusability, and support dynamic behaviour. In C, a variable is declared by specifying its data type followed by the variable name. Syntax: data_type variable_name; Example: int age = 25;

Defines variable as a named memory storage location;. States value can change during execution;. Mentions at least 2 importance points (data storage / readability / reusability / dynamic behaviour);. Gives correct C declaration syntax with data type and variable name;. Provides a valid example (e.g. int age = 25).
